sanskrit: प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते । प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते ॥
hindiTranslation: वशीभूत अन्तःकरणवाला कर्मयोगी साधक रागद्वेषसे रहित अपने वशमें की हुई इन्द्रियोंके द्वारा विषयोंका सेवन करता हुआ अन्तःकरणकी निर्मलता को प्राप्त हो जाता है ।  निर्मलता प्राप्त होनेपर साधकके सम्पूर्ण दुःखोंका नाश हो जाता है और ऐसे शुद्ध चित्तवाले साधककी बुद्धि निःसन्देह बहुत जल्दी परमात्मामें स्थिर हो जाती है ॥
englishTranslation: In inner clarity, all suffering falls away. For one whose mind is clear, understanding settles quickly.
essence: Clear seeing ends suffering and lets understanding settle at once.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: When the mind becomes calm and clear, suffering drops away and understanding becomes steady very quickly.
simpleInsight: There is relief in realizing that peace of mind is not a luxury; it is the doorway out of suffering.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between grief and duty. Krishna keeps tracing the chain backward: craving, anger, confusion. Here he shows the other direction too — when the mind becomes clear, suffering falls away and the mind can settle in truth.
modernRelevance: You finally stop arguing with a message you already know is true. The tightness in your chest loosens, and the next right thought arrives without force.
translationLiteral: प्रसादे (in clarity/serenity) / सर्वदुःखानाम् (of all sorrows) / हानिः (ending) / अस्य (of this one) / उपजायते (arises) । / प्रसन्नचेतसः (of one with a clear mind) / हि (indeed) / आशु (quickly) / बुद्धिः (understanding) / पर्यवतिष्ठते (becomes firmly established)
insight: It shifts attention from emotional turbulence to the clarity that dissolves it.

quoteVariants.text: Inner clarity ends suffering. A clear mind settles fast.
A clear mind loses its suffering first.
